title: Digital Connection vs. Mental Well-being
content: This is the title slide for a presentation examining the relationship between digital/social media connection and mental well-being. It is presented by Li Wei, a Year 2 Sociology student, for Global Studies 201.

title: The Ubiquitous Landscape
content: This slide establishes Gen Z's identity as digital natives who experience life through an online lens. It presents usage statistics for popular social media platforms and frames constant connectivity as an indispensable modern reality.
key_claims: Gen Z are recognized as Digital Natives, experiencing life through an online lens
Constant connectivity is essentially indispensable in the modern era

title: Usage and Anxiety Statistics
content: This slide presents bar chart data linking heavy social media usage to negative mental health outcomes, sourced from a 2025 Mental Health Statistics Report. It shows high usage rates alongside reported anxiety/sadness and increased depression risk.
key_claims: 87% of users report usage exceeding 2 hours per day
There is a 70% increase in depression risk factor

title: Psychological Manifestations
content: This slide outlines three key psychological effects of social media use: FOMO, social comparison, and cyberbullying. Each effect is illustrated with an icon and brief description of its impact on mental health.
key_claims: FOMO Effect: fear of missing out creates significant stress and anxiety regarding social inclusion
Comparison: constant utilization of curated feeds leads to negative self-image and low self-esteem issues
Cyberbullying: nearly 87% of teens have experienced some form of online harassment or negative interactions

title: Strategic Mitigation Efforts
content: This slide proposes three strategies to address the negative mental health effects of social media: digital detox, user awareness, and platform responsibility. Each strategy is represented with an icon and brief explanation.
key_claims: Digital Detox: implementing time limits is a crucial strategy for emotional balance
Awareness: education regarding platform algorithms is essentially significant
Platform Responsibility: companies should prioritize user well-being as a primary imperative
